Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Request For Dismissal Without Prejudice Of The Class Action Complaint In Its Entirety As To Defendant 1330 Jones Street Llc Only; Declaration Of Jaime Castaneda In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LANCE CUNNINGHAM, INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER MEMBERS OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC SIMILARLY SITUATED Request For Dismissal Without Prejudice Of The Class Action Complaint In Its Entirety As To Defendant 1330 Jones Street Llc Only.

Plaintiff Lance Cunningham's unopposed Request For Dismissal Without Prejudice Of The Class Action Complaint In Its Entirety As To Defendant 1330 Jones Street LLC Only is GRANTED AND THE COURT ISSUES RELATED ORDERS.

Good cause appearing and consistent with California Rules of Court, rule 3.770, the court orders that Plaintiff may dismiss without prejudice the class action claims in their entirety only as to Defendant 1330 Jones Street LLC. The record demonstrates that dismissal will not prejudice class members, thus no notice to the class is required. Plaintiff must file a request for dismissal on the Judicial Council form and should include a copy of this order with that submission.

The parties are advised that the court understands this case to be complex under CRC 3.400 et seq, yet the parties have not yet applied for complex designation. The court orders the parties forthwith to meet and confer regarding complex designation and pursue complex designation under California Rules of Court, rule 3.403(b) and San Francisco Local Rule, rule 3.5(C)(2)&(3). If any motion is noticed for this department in the future, the parties must file a joint statement regarding the status of complex designation at the time the motion is filed.

Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
